authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR Part 98 - Child Care and Development Fund
authoritative_sources.publisher: Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (U.S. Department of Health and Human Services)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 98 is the primary federal operating rule for subsidy-backed child care. It is authoritative for parent-facing consumer education, complaint visibility, health and safety posture, and program information that shape child-care family-engagement workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: intake context review, family-facing communication boundaries, and complaint-sensitive escalation

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR 1302.34 - Parent and family engagement in education and child development services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Office of Head Start regulation is authoritative for how early-childhood programs communicate with families about child development, screening, progress, and home learning support.

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR 1302.50 - Family engagement
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This regulation is authoritative for relationship-based family engagement expectations and keeps the lane grounded in early-childhood family partnership work rather than generic customer-service behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR 1302.52 - Family partnership services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This regulation is the clearest public source for family strengths and needs assessment, family-goal documentation, and community referral follow-up, making it directly relevant to family-engagement execution and handoff work.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: goal tracking, referral handoff, and completion evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: Using the Parent, Family, and Community Engagement Framework in Early Childhood Systems to Improve Outcomes for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.publisher: Child Care Technical Assistance Network, Office of Child Care
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Office of Child Care publishes this guidance to operationalize family engagement in early-childhood systems, so it is authoritative for workflow terminology, handoff design, and cross-team coordination expectations in child care.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: cross-team coordination and family-engagement process design

authoritative_sources.title: Parent, Family, and Community Engagement Framework
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Office of Head Start PFCE Framework is the official family-engagement operating model for early-childhood settings and is directly tied to coordination, referral, and relationship-building workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: family relationship management and multi-owner coordination

authoritative_sources.title: Caring for Our Children Basics
authoritative_sources.publisher: Administration for Children and Families, Office of Child Care
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFOCB is the federal child-care health and safety baseline used to inform licensing and CCDF implementation, making it authoritative for records, incident communication, release safety, and evidence-handling expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: evidence sufficiency, release-safety checks, and completion gating

workflow_stages.name: family-engagement intake and prerequisite confirmation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm that the child, household, program, authorized-contact, and tenant-policy context is complete enough to begin family-engagement work safely.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: active outreach, conference, and support-plan execution
workflow_stages.description: Track outreach attempts, family conferences, attendance concerns, developmental or behavioral support follow-up, and family-goal activity using child-care records and child-development context.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: referral handoff and dependency management
workflow_stages.description: Prepare and verify community-resource or adjacent-lane handoffs, making sure releases, referral details, preferred-contact data, and owner transitions are explicit.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: exception handling and source-conflict escalation
workflow_stages.description: Handle missing permissions, custody disputes, subsidy or policy questions, safety disclosures, and public-versus-tenant policy conflicts through explicit escalation rather than improvised synthesis.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: completion evidence review and closure control
workflow_stages.description: Mark work complete only when the required family-contact, conference, referral, or closure records are present and systems of record do not materially conflict.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: authorized contact, release-of-information, and custody or guardianship records
family conference notes, family-goal plans, outreach logs, and preferred-language or contact-preference records
attendance and chronic-absence follow-up records, child profile and screening summaries, and transition plans
community referral forms, partner acknowledgment records, and subsidy or fee-assistance context
incident, complaint, behavior, and health or safety communication logs
completion checklists, case dispositions, and handoff summaries
systems_of_record: subsidy, eligibility, or case portal when applicable
licensing, compliance, or incident tracking system

allowed: Classify family-engagement work as in-scope, blocked, rework, handoff-ready, or complete based on documented execution state and evidence.
Request or validate the minimum tenant facts, source records, and permissions required to continue safely.
Prepare evidence-backed execution updates, exception summaries, completion records, and handoff packets without overstating authority.
escalate: Custody, release-rights, disclosure, subsidy, complaint, or policy-interpretation conflicts that materially change the answer.
Child-safety, mandated-reporting, clinical, mental-health, or disability-support concerns that require specialized authority.
Ambiguous ownership across teaching, family-services, subsidy, compliance, or external community-resource lanes.
refuse: Disclose child or family information without required authorization or authorized-contact evidence.
Mark work complete without family-contact, referral, conference, or closure evidence.
Decide custody, subsidy, safety, clinical, legal, or licensing outcomes as though this lane owned them.

domain_failure_modes: family-engagement work proceeds without confirming who may receive information or participate, causing unsafe disclosure or pickup handling
generic customer-service language replaces child-care-specific records such as attendance follow-up, family conferences, developmental referrals, and authorized-contact controls
community-resource handoffs happen without release authorization, child needs summary, or preferred-contact data, causing referral failure
a family-engagement case is marked complete without documented contact attempts, conference notes, or referral closure evidence
teacher, family-services, subsidy, and compliance owners each assume another team owns the next step and the case is dropped
safety, custody, subsidy, or policy conflicts are improvised instead of escalated to the proper authority

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
Ownership is ambiguous across multiple teams
A handoff is attempted without the required context

unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public federal sources establish the family-engagement and health and safety baseline, but state licensing rules still control exact notice timing, incident forms, and custody documentation requirements. The pack therefore requires jurisdiction retrieval before asserting state-specific notice or release rules.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Program-model variance between Head Start, subsidy-funded centers, and private-pay child care
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Head Start regulations provide the richest public family-partnership workflow baseline, but not every child-care tenant uses the same forms or goal-planning process. The pack treats the Head Start structure as a public reference and requires tenant adaptation for the actual document set.

notes: The lane stays child-care-specific by centering authorized-contact, attendance, family-conference, family-goal, referral, and child-development communication records instead of generic account or support tickets.

The lane stops before custody decisions, subsidy determinations, mandated-reporting judgment, clinical or mental-health advice, and licensing or legal signoff even when family-engagement work is urgent.
